name: Webinar Registration → Indoctrination → Reminders → Live → Replay → Stack Close
summary: The full follow-up wrapped around a LIVE Perfect Webinar funnel: registration confirmation -> multi-day indoctrination (Soap Opera Sequence to bond registrants to the Attractive Character and raise show-up rate) -> show-up reminders (24h / 1h / 'we're live') -> attendance-segmented replay + cart-close emails using the Perfect Webinar Stack and the DotCom Secrets 3 Closes (Emotion -> Logic -> Fear).

entry_conditions: Contact registered for a specific live webinar session (tag `webinar-registered` + session/date field)
exit_conditions: Contact buys the webinar offer (tag `webinar-purchased`) -> exit selling steps, route to onboarding
segmentation_inputs: Showed up live vs no-show (tag from webinar platform / 'click to join' link)
Stayed to the close / pitch vs left early (optional, if platform reports it)
Clicked the offer/buy link but did not purchase

sequence.beat: Immediate confirmation + set expectations (Soap Opera Email #1: 'Set the Stage'). Tell them what's coming and to watch for the daily emails; add to calendar.
sequence.body_framework: Soap Opera Email #1. Welcome from the Attractive Character, set the date/time, the ONE promise of the webinar (the big domino / one thing), open a curiosity loop ('tomorrow I'll show you why X is the only thing that matters'), tell them to whitelist + add to calendar. SMS = short confirm + calendar link.

sequence.step: Emails 1-3 — Soap Opera Sequence (High Drama -> Epiphany -> Hidden Benefits)
sequence.beat: Bond registrant to the Attractive Character and pre-frame the webinar's core promise so they SHOW UP warm and believing. Each email opens a loop that pulls to the next, leading to webinar day.
sequence.delay: One per day between registration and the live date (compress to 1-2 if reg window is short)
sequence.subject_angle: Chaptered like the Soap Opera ('Ch. 2 of 5: the day everything changed')
sequence.body_framework: Soap Opera Sequence Emails #2-#4: (2) start at HIGH DRAMA then backstory to the wall the reader is also at; (3) the EPIPHANY that ties to the webinar's big idea; (4) HIDDEN BENEFITS of attending. Every email reminds them of the date/time and reinforces why this webinar is the answer to their #1 problem.
sequence.copy_notes: Epiphany Bridge story is the engine here — give them the same epiphany you had so they arrive pre-sold on the idea, not the product.

sequence.beat: Maximize show-up rate. Reminders are logistics + curiosity, not pitch.
sequence.delay: 24 hours before · 1 hour before · at start time
sequence.channel: Email + SMS (SMS critical for the 1h and live-now)
sequence.subject_angle: "Tomorrow: [big promise]" / "Starting in 1 hour" / "We're LIVE — join now"
sequence.body_framework: Soap Opera Email #5 energy (Urgency + CTA) applied to attendance: restate the single most valuable thing they'll learn, the time in their zone, and the one-click JOIN link. The 'We're LIVE' message is SMS-first with the direct join URL.

sequence.step: Replay emails — branch by no-show vs attended-but-didn't-buy
sequence.beat: Recover no-shows and give attendees a chance to re-watch; reopen the cart for a limited replay window with countdown scarcity.
sequence.delay: Replay sent within hours of the live event; replay window typically 24-72h with countdown

branches.no_show: "Sorry we missed you — here's the replay (expires soon)" — re-sell the promise, embed replay with cart-close countdown.
branches.attended_no_buy: "Did you catch the [offer] at the end?" — go straight to the Stack recap + the 3 Closes.

sequence.body_framework: Replay page mirrors the Webinar Funnel recipe: replay embed + cart-close countdown timer creates real scarcity.

sequence.beat: Drive the sale before the cart closes using the DotCom Secrets 3 Closes over the replay window.
sequence.delay: Spread across the replay/cart-open window (e.g. day of replay, mid-window, final day)
sequence.body_framework: 3 Closes framework: (1) EMOTION close — retell the Epiphany Bridge / transformation story, future-pace the result, rebuild desire; (2) LOGIC close — present THE STACK (Perfect Webinar): list every component of the offer with its standalone value, total it, then reveal the price so the deal is logically undeniable ('dollars for dimes'); restate the guarantee; (3) FEAR close — honest urgency: the cart/bonuses/price genuinely expire at the cart-close datetime; what they lose by not acting. Final SMS at the deadline.
sequence.copy_notes: The Stack slide is built cumulatively (offer + bonus + bonus + ...), value totaled, THEN price revealed — straight from Expert Secrets' Perfect Webinar close.

copy_persona.primary: DotCom Secrets + Expert Secrets (Russell Brunson) — Soap Opera Sequence for indoctrination, the Epiphany Bridge story engine, the Perfect Webinar 'Stack', and the 3 Closes for the close.
scripts_used: Soap Opera Sequence (DotCom Secrets Secret #7): Email #1 Set the Stage, #2 High Drama + Backstory, #3 Epiphany, #4 Hidden Benefits, #5 Urgency & CTA — open/close loops to pull registrant to webinar day
Epiphany Bridge (Expert Secrets) — the origin story that gives the prospect the same epiphany, told in indoctrination + Emotion close
The Perfect Webinar Stack (Expert Secrets) — cumulative offer build + value total + price reveal, used in the Logic close
3 Closes (DotCom Secrets Follow-Up Funnel): Emotion -> Logic -> Fear, sequenced across the cart-open replay window
Honest urgency only — Soap Opera rule 'fake urgency backfires; it must be real'
copy_persona.support_persona: jim-edwards-copywriting-secrets — sentence-level subject lines (curiosity, fear/desire headlines) and the honest-scarcity discipline on the Fear close.
copy_persona.structural_persona: russell-brunson-the-funnel-hackers-cookbook — Webinar Funnel recipe: registration -> confirmation/indoctrination -> live event -> replay/urgency page with cart-close countdown; Drop-Off Automation for no-shows.

ghl_build.engine: Skill 44 (caf) builds the GoHighLevel workflow(s) from an ordered build-plan; for a date-based live webinar use an Event/Appointment-style trigger or a fixed-send schedule keyed to the session datetime custom field.
ghl_build.workflow_name: FSF — Live Webinar: Reg -> Indoctrinate -> Remind -> Replay -> Close

trigger_config.ghl_trigger: Form Submitted = webinar registration page (or Tag Added `webinar-registered`)
trigger_config.session_field: Store webinar datetime in a custom field so reminder waits compute 24h/1h-before relative to it

actions_in_order: 1. Send Email + SMS — Confirmation (Set the Stage); add tag `webinar-registered`
2. Send Email — Indoctrination #1 (next day)  [Wait 1 day between each]
5. Wait until 24h before session -> Send Email + SMS reminder
6. Wait until 1h before session -> Send SMS reminder
7. Wait until session start -> Send SMS 'We're LIVE' with join link
9. If/Else attendance tag (`webinar-attended` vs `webinar-noshow`): branch replay copy
10. Send Email + SMS — Replay (branched) with cart-close countdown
13. Send Email — Close #2 LOGIC (The Stack + value total + price)
15. Send Email + SMS — Close #3 FEAR (honest deadline)
ghl_build.goal_event: `webinar-purchased` -> exit all selling steps; route contact to the post-purchase onboarding workflow.
if_else_branches: clicked-buy-link-no-purchase -> a tighter 'you were so close' nudge
purchased at any point -> Goal exit
ghl_build.notes: Attendance tags come from the webinar platform webhook or a 'click to join' trigger-link click. The cart-close countdown on the replay page must reflect the SAME deadline the Fear close cites — keep them in sync so urgency is real.

books: DotCom Secrets (Soap Opera Sequence Secret #7; Follow-Up Funnel 3 Closes Emotion/Logic/Fear)
Expert Secrets (Perfect Webinar, The Stack, Epiphany Bridge)
The Funnel Hacker's Cookbook (Webinar Funnel recipe: registration -> confirmation/indoctrination -> live -> replay/urgency with countdown; Drop-Off Automation for no-shows)
Copywriting Secrets — Jim Edwards (subject-line/headline craft, honest scarcity)
source.fidelity_note: Sequence beats are the verbatim Soap Opera email purposes (#1-#5), the documented 3 Closes order, the Webinar Funnel page recipe, and the Perfect Webinar Stack. No invented steps.

flexibility.principle: This template is a recommended default and a resource — it is NEVER a rule or a requirement.
flexibility.mode_1_user_has_explicit_desire: Do exactly what the user wants. This template is an optional reference only; it is never imposed or overridden onto their choice.
flexibility.mode_2_user_is_unsure: Suggest this proven template and explain why it works, but let the user decide.
flexibility.mode_3_user_wants_it_handled: Build the whole automation from this template.
flexibility.note: Always overridable, mixable, customizable, or ignorable. It assists; it does not dominate.
